method: Row left-anchoring (left-justified layout) test, L2 spatial. Per doc: cluster non-divider glyphs into y-band rows (tol=0.6*median glyph height); row LEFT-edge=min glyph-left-x, RIGHT-edge=max glyph-right-x; medw=median glyph width; lsd=pstdev(LEFT-edges)/medw, rsd=pstdev(RIGHT-edges)/medw; doc asymmetry a=rsd-lsd (positive=>left tighter=>left-anchored). Usable doc: >=6 non-divider bbox glyphs AND >=3 rows. S_obs=median(a) over usable docs. NULL=edge-exchangeability (sign-symmetric): per doc sample s in {+1,-1} uniformly, contribute s*(rsd-lsd), S=median(contributions); one-sided perm p=frac(null S>=S_obs); >=5000 draws. Positive control SYNTHETIC, three arms (left-justified detect / centered false-positive / right-justified direction).
result: Global rows are significantly left-anchored (S_obs=+0.459, perm_p=0.0002): row LEFT edges align tightly (median lsd=0.664) while RIGHT edges are ragged (median rsd=1.248), 65.5% of docs left-tighter-than-right. The edge-swap sign-symmetric null (median~0) is rejected. However the cross-site pattern is heterogeneous: Haghia Triada strongly left-anchored (S_site=+0.518, p=0.0002), Zakros directionally left-anchored but NOT significant at 0.05 (S_site=+0.440, p=0.10), and Khania at chance (S_site=-0.029, p=0.55, frac=0.50). Only 1 of 3 testable sites is significant => SITE_LOCAL, not cross-site.
key_findings: GLOBAL rows are significantly left-anchored: S_obs=+0.459 (median asymmetry rsd-lsd), perm_p=0.0002 vs the edge-exchangeability sign-swap null (null median ~0). Row LEFT edges are tight (median lsd=0.664) while RIGHT edges are ragged (median rsd=1.248) — a left-justified layout signature.
65.5% of usable docs (129/197) have left-edge spread smaller than right-edge spread (frac_left_tighter=0.655), consistent with a left margin that rows align to.
Haghia Triada (the largest site, n=124) drives the global signal strongly: S_site=+0.518, perm_p=0.0002, frac=0.645 — clearly left-anchored.

Cross-site heterogeneity blocks a cross-site verdict: Khania is at chance (S_site=-0.029, p=0.55, frac=0.50 — edges exchangeable) and Zakros is directionally left-anchored but NOT significant at 0.05 (S_site=+0.440, p=0.10). Only 1 of 3 testable sites is significant.

Positive control (SYNTHETIC) passed all three arms: left-justified detected with power_est=1.0, centered false-positive rate=0.05 (<=0.10), right-justified correctly NOT flagged as left-anchored (S_obs<0).

Combined with E079 (regular horizontal glyph spacing) and E080 (regular vertical line spacing), the global result completes a left-justified 2D ruled grid at the corpus level — but the anchoring is site-local, not uniform across all sites.
successor_hypotheses: E082: Why is Khania NOT left-anchored (edges exchangeable, frac=0.50)? Test whether Khania docs use a different layout convention (centered or right-anchored) or whether its smaller/different tablet corpus has distinct scribal practice — a possible regional layout variant.

E083: Re-test Zakros left-anchoring with a larger Zakros sample (currently n=21, p=0.10 borderline) to determine if its directional S_site=+0.44 reaches significance, or if Zakros is genuinely intermediate between HT and Khania.

E084: Test whether the left margin position itself is consistent ACROSS documents within a site (a shared physical margin x-coordinate), beyond per-doc left-edge alignment — i.e. is there a site-standardized left margin line?

E085: Decompose left-anchoring by document SUPPORT (tablet vs other) and row length — are short rows (few glyphs) more or less left-anchored than long rows, and does support type modulate the anchoring strength?

E086: Test for a TOP-anchoring analogue in the vertical axis (do columns/blocks share a common top edge with ragged bottoms), completing the 2D anchoring picture orthogonal to E081's horizontal left-anchoring.

E087: Quantify the left-margin alignment strength as a continuous per-site score and correlate with E079/E080 spacing regularity to test whether the most regularly-spaced docs are also the most strongly left-anchored (a unified grid-quality factor).
non_circular: NON-CIRCULAR. Signs treated as OPAQUE IDs (no value, no reading, no meaning); only glyph bbox positions (row left/right x-edges) enter the metric. Reading DIRECTION is a corpus assumption, but left-ANCHORING (physical margin alignment) is a separate geometric fact tested by an edge-swap sign-symmetric null on geometry only — swapping which edge is 'left' vs 'right' per doc is a pure geometry operation independent of any reading. The null (edge-exchangeability) makes no use of linguistic content. DISTINCT from E079 (horizontal glyph SPACING) and E080 (vertical line SPACING): E081 tests edge ALIGNMENT/anchoring, orthogonal to spacing.
deviations: Centered positive-control arm was constructed with mirror-symmetric left/right edges around each row's center (draw one half-width deviation d; left=center-d, right=center+d) so that edges are genuinely exchangeable by construction; a naive centered placement biases rsd>lsd because per-glyph width jitter accumulates only on the right edge. This makes the false-positive target fair; it does not affect the real-data analysis or the left/right detect arms. Documented in machinery.py make_synthetic().
perm_p uses an add-one correction (ge+1)/(n_draws+1) for finite-sample safety; verified stable across seeds (global p<=0.00005 at 20k draws; Zakros p~0.10 stable).
